chapter_context.summary: Hiram of Tyre sends cedar and builders to David; David builds his house, lists his sons born in Jerusalem, and defeats the invading Philistines twice at God's instruction.

outline.text: Hiram of Tyre Sends Wood and Builders; David's Household
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hiram of Tyre Sends Wood and Builders; David's Household (14:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.
This section focuses on David's Sons Born in Jerusalem (14:3-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David Consults God and Defeats the Philistines Twice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Consults God and Defeats the Philistines Twice (14:8-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 14, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
